meta.regionFocus: Iran, Persky zaliv, Irak, Syrie, Izrael a okoli
meta.editorialNote: Tahle sada je pripravena pro zive nasazeni. Aktualni sablonove zaznamy slouzi jako struktura pro redakcni workflow a maji byt nahrazeny pouze overenymi udalostmi.

watchlist.label: Letecke utoky a protivzdusna obrana
watchlist.description: Sledovat zasahy, presuny techniky, uzavreni vzdusneho prostoru a dopady na civilni infrastrukturu.

watchlist.label: Politicka rozhodnuti a diplomaticke reakce
watchlist.description: Vladni prohlaseni, rezoluce, sankce, krizova jednani a vystoupeni armadnich mluvcich.

watchlist.label: Civilni dopady
watchlist.description: Evakuace, preruseni dodavek, omezeni internetu, zdravotni rizika a bezpecnost civilistu.

incidents.title: Iranian missile-and-drone attack wounds U.S. troops at Prince Sultan Air Base
incidents.summary: Iran fired ballistic missiles and drones at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging aircraft and injuring U.S. personnel.
incidents.body: AP reported that Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones at Prince Sultan Air Base on 27 March 2026. Follow-up reporting said at least 15 U.S. troops were injured, including several seriously, and aircraft at the base were damaged.
incidents.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

incidents.title: Strike hits fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport
incidents.summary: An Iranian strike on 25 March hit a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport and triggered a fire.
incidents.body: AP reported that one of Iran's attacks on Gulf Arab countries on 25 March 2026 struck a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport. The strike highlighted the growing exposure of Gulf civil aviation infrastructure.

incidents.title: Debris from intercepted Iranian drone sparks fire at Fujairah oil facility
incidents.summary: Debris from an intercepted Iranian drone struck an oil facility in Fujairah and started a fire.
incidents.body: AP reported that after Iranian warnings about major UAE ports, debris from an intercepted drone hit an oil facility in Fujairah on 14 March 2026. The incident extended the war's direct impact to critical Gulf port and oil infrastructure.

incidents.title: U.S. says it destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels in the Strait of Hormuz campaign
incidents.summary: U.S. officials said 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels were destroyed during operations tied to threats against shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
incidents.body: AP reported on 10 March 2026 that the U.S. military said it destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels as Tehran threatened to block oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz. Open-source confirmation of the full claim remained limited at the time of reporting.
incidents.verificationNote: Important official claim with major strategic implications, but independent corroboration in open sources was limited at the time of reporting.

incidents.title: STENA IMPERATIVE struck by projectiles while berthed in Bahrain
incidents.summary: Official maritime advisories recorded the STENA IMPERATIVE as hit by two projectiles while berthed in Bahrain.
incidents.body: JMIC and IMO advisories reported that the U.S.-flagged tanker STENA IMPERATIVE was struck in the Port Salman / HIDD area in the early hours of 2 March 2026. The strike caused a fire, killed one shipyard worker and seriously injured two others.

incidents.title: Tanker MKD VYOM hit by projectile/USV north of Muscat
incidents.summary: The tanker MKD VYOM was struck north of Muscat in an incident later assessed as involving an unmanned surface vessel.
incidents.body: Official maritime advisories recorded the Marshall Islands-flagged crude tanker MKD VYOM as struck about 45 nautical miles north of Muscat on 1 March 2026. Reporting later assessed the strike as involving an unmanned surface vessel and confirmed an engine-room fire with one fatality.
incidents.location: 45 NM north of Muscat, Gulf of Oman

mapPoint.label: Gulf of Oman north of Muscat

incidents.title: Israeli use of compromised Tehran camera network during opening strikes
incidents.summary: Reporting indicates Israeli operators used compromised Tehran surveillance cameras to help track targets during the opening strikes.
incidents.body: AP reported that Israeli operators used compromised Tehran surveillance cameras during the opening day of the war on 28 February 2026. The account cited people briefed on the operation and previous public warnings about camera compromise, but the operational details were not fully independently verified in public.
incidents.verificationNote: Reported by AP with sourcing tied to briefed officials and prior public warnings about camera compromise; operational details remain only partially corroborated in public.

incidents.title: Regional airspace closures ripple across the Gulf after the opening strikes on Iran
incidents.summary: Airspace closures and restrictions spread across the Gulf after the opening strikes on Iran, affecting multiple regional states and airlines.
incidents.body: After the opening U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran on 28 February 2026, AP reported closures over Israel, Qatar, Syria, Iran, Iraq, Kuwait and Bahrain, with a partial UAE closure. EASA later maintained a conflict-zone bulletin covering Iran and neighboring airspace, and Qatar Airways published its own operational update.
incidents.targetType: Regional airspace and civil aviation operations
